title: Intense Smoky Australian Lentil Casserole
description: A winey, molten, weeknight Australian dish featuring bacon lardon and infused oil, jain-friendly and perfect for a cozy dinner
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pan, cook the diced bacon lardon over medium heat until crispy.
Remove the bacon from the pan and set aside, leaving the grease behind.
Add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery to the pan and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Add the red wine, vegetable broth, tomato paste, and smoked paprika to the pan, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked lentils, bacon, and vegetable mixture, stirring to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and drizzle with infused oil.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned.
